frame_analyses.description: Two needle drivers positioned above trainer box, suture visible as blue 2-0 silk. Initial setup phase before suture placement begins.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments are positioned and ready; no needle grasp visible yet.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, beginning to position for entry through Penrose drain slit. Needle orientation appears appropriate for backhand entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle held at approximately 2/3 point from tip; positioning looks controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver advancing needle through drain toward first mark. Suture line visible in blue, beginning to pass through drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle entry appears perpendicular to drain axis; good technique.

frame_analyses.description: Needle has passed through drain at first mark; suture visible exiting drain with blue loop formation starting. Second needle driver positioning for exit point.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry point on drain appears close to mark 1; needle advancement controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers visible maneuvering suture through drain. Suture loop evident between instruments; both ends being handled to create proper bite.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordination between hands appears smooth; suture management adequate.

frame_analyses.description: Suture fully threaded through drain creating complete loop. Needle exiting at second mark position. Penrose drain visible with suture transversing the slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement geometry appears acceptable; loop well-formed.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments manipulating suture ends to form first knot. Right hand appearing to lead with wrapping motion. Suture ends visible outside drain field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Wrapping pattern consistent with surgeon's knot (double throw) technique. Right hand dominant.

frame_analyses.description: First throw in progress. Suture wrapped around needle driver with second wrap beginning. Tension controlled; loop formation visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double wrap clearly visible; knot formation appears secure.

frame_analyses.description: First throw completed and tightened. Instruments repositioning for second throw (first single throw). Left hand now taking lead, indicating proper hand switching.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean transition; hand switch from right to left demonstrated. Knot 1 appears secure.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw (first single throw) being executed. Left hand leading with wrapping. Suture ends positioned for single wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Single wrap formation; alternating hand pattern maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw completed. Instruments repositioning for third throw. Right hand taking lead again, confirming alternating hand pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand alternation demonstrated correctly across throws. Knot 2 appears secure.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw (second single throw) being formed. Right hand leading with wrap. All three throws now complete.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final throw forming cleanly; knot sequence complete (surgeon's knot + 2 single throws).

frame_analyses.description: Scissors or cutting instrument visible approaching completed knot on drain. Both suture ends present above drain surface, ready for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot position on drain appears adequate; ready for cut phase.

frame_analyses.description: Suture being cut. One end of suture visible being divided. Instrument performing cut near knot base.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting positioned to leave minimal tail length.

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed task. Drain with knot in place, suture ends cut and visible on drain surface. Both suture ends appear to be inside trainer box as required.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears complete. Drain integrity maintained; slit appears closed around suture.

penalties.description: Entry point at mark 1 appears approximately 1.5mm lateral to exact mark position based on visual inspection of frame 4
Exit point at mark 2 appears approximately 1mm superior to exact mark position

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap, appropriate tension, knot sits well on drain
Single wrap executed cleanly, proper hand switch demonstrated
Final single throw completed with right hand, alternating pattern maintained throughout

drain_assessment.notes: Drain slit appears properly closed around suture; no visible gaps or tissue damage

confidence_rationale: Completion time is well-observed from clear frame progression. Knot sequence and hand switching are clearly visible. Suture deviation measurements are estimated from camera angles that are oblique to the entry/exit points, reducing confidence in mm-level precision. Drain integrity appears intact but exact gap presence difficult to assess with 100% certainty due to camera angle. Hand switching pattern is unambiguously demonstrated across all three throws.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation at mark 1 — camera angle is not perpendicular to drain entry
Exact mm deviation at mark 2 — exit point assessment limited by viewing angle
Whether suture ends were cut inside or outside trainer — final frames do not clearly show interior of box
technique_summary: Solid intermediate-to-expert performance demonstrating proper knot sequence (surgeon's knot + 2 alternating single throws), consistent hand switching, and good suture management. Completion time of 152 seconds places performance at lower-intermediate level. Suture placement deviations are minor (<2mm each) and within acceptable tolerance. Drain integrity fully preserved.
strengths: Correct knot sequence executed: surgeon's knot (double throw) followed by two single throws
Consistent hand switching demonstrated across all three throws (right → left → right)
Smooth instrument coordination throughout suture placement and knot tying
Clean knot formation with appropriate tension at each stage
Drain integrity preserved; no avulsion or detachment
improvement_suggestions: Reduce suture placement time from 29s to ~15s through deliberate practice of atraumatic needle advancement
Tighten individual throw execution to 15-20s per throw (currently 23-34s range)
Work on precise mark targeting to achieve <0.5mm deviation
Practice continuous motion during knot tying to reduce total time toward 80-100s expert range
